figures.caption: Fig. 5. Visualization of surface reconstruction on TanksAndTemples [Knapitsch et al. 2017] dataset. We obtain the GTmesh through Poisson-Disk reconstruction on the GT point clouds for reference. We produce complete and high-fidelity surfaces from only 5 input views.

Fig. 7. Visualization of surface reconstruction and novel view synthesis on DL3DV [Ling et al. 2024] dataset with 6 input views. Our method successfully reconstructs and renders complete scenes with high-fidelity.
Table 2. Numerical comparisons of novel view synthesis with 6-view inputs on DL3DV [Ling et al. 2024] dataset.
Fig. 8. Comparison of our method with other camera-controlled video generation methods. We achieve significantly more consistent results obeying ground truth geometries.

Fig. 2. Overview of our optimization framework. Given sparse input views, we sample novel camera trajectories and employ a camera-controlled video diffusion model (VDM) with our geometry-guided denoising strategy to generate additional views. In the initialization stage, RGB and mask images rendered from point cloud are used as VDM inputs, and the generated views are used to complete the initial point cloud. In the training stage, Gaussian-rendered RGBs and mesh-rendered masks are used as inputs, and the generated views are used to expand the training view set. The newly added point clouds and mesh surfaces are highlighted in blue.

Fig. 3. Illustration of our visibility-based camera pose sampling strategy. Camera trajectories are constructed on a spherical surface, and the visibility of keyframes is evaluated accordingly. For example, (a) is discarded due to excessive unseen region coverage, while (b) is discarded because of occlusion by the wall.
Fig. 10. Ablation study on our stage-wise denoising strategy.

Fig. 4. Illustration of our geometry-guided denoising for 3D consistent video generation. At each step, we blend the noisy latents 𝑥𝑡−1 with the reference inversion 𝑥𝑟𝑒𝑓 𝑡−1 using𝑀 (𝑡 ) to guide the denoising direction toward underlying geometry.
